title: Change Tab Coverage
description:
Studio UI for **Change Tab Coverage**: implementation footprint for an active change — manifest
**accepted links** and **tracked files**, merged with change-scoped **code-graph** coverage.
Studio UI component: **Change Tab Coverage**.
context:
Studio UI for **Change Tab Coverage**: implementation footprint for an active change — manifest
**accepted links** and **tracked files**, merged with change-scoped **code-graph** coverage.
Studio UI component: **Change Tab Coverage**.

rules.requirement: coverage tab loads manifest tracking and graph view
rules:
While this change tab is visible for an **active** change, the view MUST load:

1. `getImplementationReview(name)` — manifest `implementationTracking.links` and `implementationTracking.trackedFiles`
2. `getChangeGraphView(name)` — per-spec `coveredFiles` / `coveredSymbols`

Both MUST refresh on tab-scoped poll ticks. Polling MUST pause when the tab is hidden or the window lacks focus. Archived changes MUST NOT call these endpoints (read-only messaging only).

The visible tab label MUST be `Coverage`.

For **drafted**, **discarded**, and **archived** changes, implementation coverage is not available
in Studio v1. The Coverage tab MUST be hidden (not selectable) for these sections.

The UI MUST render one card per spec ID (union of change `specIds`, manifest links, and graph coverage), ordered per [`ui:design-system`](../design-system/spec.md). Within each card, subsections appear in order:

1. **Accepted links** — manifest links for that spec, merged with matching code-graph files/symbols for the link file
2. **Graph (not linked)** — graph symbols first, then graph files, for that spec not covered by an accepted link (omitted when empty)
3. **Tracked · resolved**, then **open**, then **ignored** — tracked files assigned to that spec when uniquely matchable via links or graph paths

Tracked files that match zero or multiple specs MUST appear under a final **Tracked files (unassigned)** section.
Spec cards with no accepted links, no graph-only rows, and no tracked files MUST NOT be rendered.
Components MUST consume data through `SpecdDataPort` hooks and MUST NOT import `@specd/core`.

rules.requirement: view surfaces loading and error states
rules: While requests are in flight or fail, the UI MUST show loading indicators and human-readable errors.

scenarios.requirement: coverage tab loads manifest tracking and graph view
scenarios.name: Coverage tab calls review and graph endpoints
then: AND waits for both before rendering sections

scenarios.requirement: coverage tab loads manifest tracking and graph view
scenarios.name: Hidden tab pauses polling
given: Coverage tab was visible then hidden
then: no tab-scoped review or graph request is sent

scenarios.requirement: coverage tab loads manifest tracking and graph view
scenarios.name: Archived change skips data fetch
then: UI does not call getImplementationReview or getChangeGraphView

scenarios.requirement: coverage tab loads manifest tracking and graph view
scenarios.name: Drafted change hides Coverage tab
given: drafted change open in the shell
then: Coverage is not shown as a selectable tab

scenarios.name: One card per spec with accepted links inside
given: change has links for ui:foo and core:bar
then: two spec cards are shown with specId headers
AND each card lists only links for that spec

scenarios.name: Graph not linked appears under the same spec card
given: graph coverage for ui:foo includes files without accepted links
then: Graph (not linked) subsection appears inside the ui:foo card

scenarios.name: Graph not linked shows symbols before files
given: graph coverage for one spec includes both extra symbols and extra files
then: the subsection lists graph symbols before graph files

scenarios.name: Tracked files appear under assigned spec
given: tracked file uniquely matches a link for one spec
then: file appears under that spec’s tracked subsections (resolved / open / ignored)

scenarios.name: Ambiguous tracked files use unassigned section
given: tracked file matches no spec or multiple specs
then: file appears in Tracked files (unassigned) at the bottom

scenarios.name: empty specs are omitted — primary path
when: Spec cards with no accepted links, no graph-only
then: AND no forbidden side effects occur

scenarios.name: empty specs are omitted — guard path
given: inputs that stress the requirement boundary
then: errors or skips are explicit and documented

scenarios.name: Component consumes SpecdDataPort hooks only
when: UI package dependency graph is inspected

scenarios.requirement: view surfaces loading and error states
scenarios.name: Loading shows single progress message
when: review or graph request is in flight
then: UI shows loading text before sections render

scenarios.requirement: view surfaces loading and error states
scenarios.name: Failed fetch shows human-readable error
